claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 16 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 16 and in approved education
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 16 and in approved education; Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is 16 and in approved education
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you find out if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions about your circumstances.

First, could you please tell me how many children you would like to claim Child Benefit for, and what are their names and ages?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text:
Hello! Yes, I'm interested in claiming Child Benefit and would like to know if I'm eligible.

I have one child - Alex, who is 16 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information about Alex.

Since Alex is 16 years old, I need to ask: is Alex currently in full-time education or on an approved training course? 

(Children aged 16-19 can still qualify for Child Benefit if they remain in qualifying education or training.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex is still in education - doing A-levels at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you for confirming that Alex is studying A-levels. That's helpful to know.

Now I have a few more questions to assess your eligibility:

1. First, does Alex live with you?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex lives at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. That's good to know.

Next, could you tell me about your own situation:

Are you currently living in the United Kingdom (England, Scotland, Wales, or Northern Ireland)?

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Lovely, thank you for confirming that.

One more question: Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Based on the information you've provided and the eligibility rules, I now have enough details to make an assessment for Alex.

Let me summarise what you've told me:
- Alex is 16 years old
- Alex is in full-time approved education (A-levels)
- Alex lives with you
- You live in the UK (Manchester)
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great news! Based on the information you've provided, **you appear to be eligible for Child Benefit for Alex**.

Here's a summary of why:

**For Alex:**
- ✅ Alex is 16 years old and in approved full-time education (A-levels), which qualifies until they turn 20 as long as they remain in approved education or training
- ✅ Alex lives with you, so you are responsible for them
- ✅ You live in the UK
- ✅ No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex

You should be able to make a claim for Child Benefit. Just be aware that if Alex leaves approved education or training, or starts working 24 hours or more per week, you would need to inform the Child Benefit Office as this could affect your eligibility.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex is 16 years old and currently in approved education (studying A-levels). According to Child Benefit rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if your child is under 20 and stays in approved education or training. Alex lives with the claimant, making them responsible for the child. The claimant resides in the UK (Manchester), and no one else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex. All eligibility criteria are met.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant appears to be eligible for Child Benefit for Alex. They are responsible for a child who is 16 years old and in approved full-time education (A-levels), they live in the UK, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for this child.

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 16 years old and currently in approved education (studying A-levels). According to Child Benefit rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if your child is under 20 and stays in approved education or training. Alex lives with the claimant, making them responsible for the child. The claimant resides in the UK (Manchester), and no one else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex. All eligibility criteria are met.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant appears to be eligible for Child Benefit for Alex. They are responsible for a child who is 16 years old and in approved full-time education (A-levels), they live in the UK, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for this child.
